title: pasta system tests: cleanup, fixes, sanity check
body:
Cleanup:
- typo fix, a misspelled variable resulting in test being NOP
- remove unnecessary variable (followup to #19044)

New test:
- opportunistically add a test for new `default_rootless_network_cmd` in containers.conf (followup to #19032). Because it just made me queasy to test that only in e2e.

New sanity check:
- in `pasta_test_do()`, the convoluted multipurpose helper function, cross-reference the test name (displayed to users) vs the function args. Basically, make sure that if the test name says TCP we don't inadvertently test UDP, same with IPv4/6 and interfaces and ranges and whatnot.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

First patch looks good to me.  I have no idea what the second patch is doing, but I'll take your word for it that it's useful.
Same here, I couldn't quite understand what the second patch does, but I'll take your word for it, and I'm fine with the first one.

@dgibson and @sbrivio-rh are right: the code is too hard to understand. 

The principle is DIE (Duplication Is Evil), not DIABOLICAL (Duplication Is Acceptable But OnLy If Crosschecks Are sLapped-on). I will work on the proper fix I mentioned in comments.
